sources.label: Boston Dynamics - New Atlas unveil (CES 2026, prior news)
Korea Herald - Hyundai to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots (prior news)
TechTimes - KMWU union blocks Atlas deployment without labor deal (May 22, prior news)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since June 6 and the stock was flat today; yesterday's verdict already marked it down for the +114% YTD rerating and large-cap crowding, so a second consecutive demotion is unwarranted. Mild negative drift as the known KMWU summer-2026 labor showdown over Atlas factory entry sits unresolved.
verdicts.bull: Hyundai owns the most credible humanoid commercialization path: 25,000 committed Atlas units, targeted 30k/yr production by 2028, Mobis actuator supply, and a Google DeepMind foundation-model partnership.
verdicts.neutral: It is a $100B auto OEM where Atlas is a small slice of value; after a 114% YTD rerating the humanoid story is well known and largely priced, leaving little hidden alpha on this page.
verdicts.bear: The Korean Metal Workers' Union has publicly vowed to block Atlas from factories without a labor agreement, and the robotics/autonomy affiliates carry W2.2T of accumulated equity-method losses — deployment slippage would deflate the rerating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Summer 2026 Hyundai-KMWU labor negotiation outcome clearing (or blocking) the Atlas factory deployment timeline; first RMAC fleet shipments.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Union-driven deployment delay or auto-cycle earnings weakness unwinding a crowded, post-rerating position.
